name: Sverdlovsk Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the snows and the silver of Ural mineral wealth
the lakes and rivers of the oblast and the gemstones of the Ural Mountains
the taiga forests of the Urals and the malachite for which the region is famous

description: A horizontal banner of white, blue, white and green stripes in proportions 7:9:1:3, with the broad blue stripe sandwiched between two white stripes (the upper one wide, the lower one narrow) above a narrow green stripe.
history: The flag of Sverdlovsk Oblast was officially adopted on 3 March 2005. The colour palette is intended to evoke the historic banner of Yermak Timofeyevich (the Cossack ataman who led the early Russian campaigns into the Urals and Siberia in the 1580s) and the natural environment of the Urals.

features.symbolism: the white-blue-green palette draws on the heraldic tinctures of the Ural region; the colour scheme echoes the historic banner of Yermak Timofeyevich, the Cossack who began the Russian conquest of Siberia from the Urals.
